evals.prompt: Generate a bar chart of the short links created in the past 12 months and save it as link-stats.png
evals.expected_output: A PNG file named link-stats.png in the current directory containing a bar chart with 12 monthly bars, x-axis showing month labels (e.g. 'Jun 2025'), y-axis showing integer link counts, and an appropriate title.

assertions.text: A PNG file is present in the output directory
The PNG file is larger than 10 KB (confirms a real rendered chart, not an empty file)
The filename matches the user's requested name (link-stats.png or similar)

evals.prompt: How many links did we create per month last year? Show me a chart.
evals.expected_output: A PNG bar chart covering the past 12 calendar months, with all 12 months present on the x-axis (zero-height bar for months with no links), exported as a PNG and the file path reported to the user.

assertions.text: A PNG file is present in the output directory
The PNG file is larger than 10 KB (confirms a real rendered chart)
All 12 calendar months are represented (zero-count months included, not just months with data)

evals.prompt: Export a link creation graph to /tmp/my-chart.png using the .env at the project root.
evals.expected_output: A PNG file at /tmp/my-chart.png showing 12 monthly bars; the script is invoked with --output /tmp/my-chart.png --env .env; the path is confirmed to the user.

assertions.text: A PNG file is present in the output directory
The PNG file is larger than 10 KB (confirms a real rendered chart)
The output path honors the user-specified location (/tmp/my-chart.png → copied to outputs as my-chart.png)
